The Best Cardamom Buns

@graph.name: The Best Cardamom Buns
@graph.description: This step-by-step recipe will guide you in baking the best cardamom buns right at home. Enjoy the spicy-sweet flavor of freshly ground cardamom and the pillowy texture that makes these buns an all-time favorite. This recipe yields 6 large or 12 smaller buns.

video.name: The Best Cardamom Buns
video.description: This step-by-step recipe will guide you in baking the best cardamom buns right at home. Enjoy the spicy-sweet flavor of freshly ground cardamom and the pillowy texture that makes these buns an all-time favorite.

recipeIngredient: 65 g unsalted butter (in cubes, softened at room temp)
75 g unsalted butter (softened at room temp)
3 g decorticated cardamom seeds (ground in mortar and pestle)
2 g decorticated cardamom seeds (ground in mortar and pestle)

itemListElement.text: Combine all the ingredients listed for the dough, excluding the butter, in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook.
itemListElement.name: Combine all the ingredients listed for the dough, excluding the butter, in the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with a dough hook.

itemListElement.text: Knead the dough on low to medium speed for about 10 minutes.
itemListElement.name: Knead the dough on low to medium speed for about 10 minutes.

itemListElement.text: Gradually add the cubed butter to the dough, one cube at a time, while continuing to knead.
itemListElement.name: Gradually add the cubed butter to the dough, one cube at a time, while continuing to knead.

itemListElement.text: Continue kneading for another 10-20 minutes or until the dough passes the window-pane test – it should be smooth, elastic, and able to be stretched thinly without tearing.
itemListElement.name: Continue kneading for another 10-20 minutes or until the dough passes the window-pane test – it should be smooth, elastic, and able to be stretched thinly without tearing.

itemListElement.text: Transfer the dough to a clean, lightly oiled bowl.
itemListElement.name: Transfer the dough to a clean, lightly oiled bowl.

itemListElement.text: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough proof. You have two options: Option 1. For a standard first proof, let the dough rise at around 77°F (25°C) for 60-90 minutes until it doubles in size. Option 2. For easier handling during shaping, proof the dough for about 45 minutes at around 77°F (25°C) until it rises to about 60% of its original size (where 100% would mean doubling). Transfer the dough to the fridge and let it rest for 1-2 hours or until it has fully doubled. This second rest in the fridge allows the dough to firm up slightly while continuing to rise.
itemListElement.name: Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the dough proof. You have two options: Option 1. For a standard first proof, let the dough rise at around 77°F (25°C) for 60-90 minutes until it doubles in size. Option 2. For easier handling during shaping, proof the dough for about 45 minutes at around 77°F (25°C) until it rises to about 60% of its original size (where 100% would mean doubling). Transfer the dough to the fridge and let it rest for 1-2 hours or until it has fully doubled. This second rest in the fridge allows the dough to firm up slightly while continuing to rise.

itemListElement.text: Grind the decorticated cardamom seeds in your mortar and pestle until they’re medium-fine. There’s no need to grind them too finely.
itemListElement.name: Grind the decorticated cardamom seeds in your mortar and pestle until they’re medium-fine. There’s no need to grind them too finely.

itemListElement.text: Combine all the filling ingredients in a small bowl, including the 3 grams of ground cardamom. Mix thoroughly with a fork or small spatula. Cover the bowl and set it aside at room temperature until you’re ready to shape the cardamom buns.
itemListElement.name: Combine all the filling ingredients in a small bowl, including the 3 grams of ground cardamom. Mix thoroughly with a fork or small spatula. Cover the bowl and set it aside at room temperature until you’re ready to shape the cardamom buns.

itemListElement.text: Once the dough has doubled, you're ready to shape the cardamom buns.
itemListElement.name: Once the dough has doubled, you're ready to shape the cardamom buns.

itemListElement.text: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and roll it out into a rectangle 18×14 inches (45×35 cm) long.
itemListElement.name: Transfer the dough to a lightly floured surface and roll it out into a rectangle 18×14 inches (45×35 cm) long.

itemListElement.text: Spread the filling evenly over the dough surface using an offset spatula.
itemListElement.name: Spread the filling evenly over the dough surface using an offset spatula.

itemListElement.text: Grab the shorter side with both hands and fold the dough down 2/3 of the way, then fold the remaining 1/3 over the top, like folding a letter.
itemListElement.name: Grab the shorter side with both hands and fold the dough down 2/3 of the way, then fold the remaining 1/3 over the top, like folding a letter.

itemListElement.text: Gently roll over the dough with your rolling pin, using the weight of the pin as enough pressure to seal the layers without flattening them.
itemListElement.name: Gently roll over the dough with your rolling pin, using the weight of the pin as enough pressure to seal the layers without flattening them.

itemListElement.text: Next, depending on your muffin pan, divide the dough: if using a regular muffin tin, cut it into 12 strands; for a jumbo muffin tin, divide it into 6 strands.
itemListElement.name: Next, depending on your muffin pan, divide the dough: if using a regular muffin tin, cut it into 12 strands; for a jumbo muffin tin, divide it into 6 strands.

itemListElement.text: Please refer to the images, description, and recipe video earlier in this post for step-by-step instructions on shaping braided cardamom buns. You'll find them under the "How to Shape Braided Cardamom Buns" section.
itemListElement.name: Please refer to the images, description, and recipe video earlier in this post for step-by-step instructions on shaping braided cardamom buns. You'll find them under the "How to Shape Braided Cardamom Buns" section.

itemListElement.text: Transfer the shaped cardamom buns to your muffin tin. Cover them with plastic wrap and let them proof for 20-40 minutes until they've visibly increased in size and look nice and puffy.
itemListElement.name: Transfer the shaped cardamom buns to your muffin tin. Cover them with plastic wrap and let them proof for 20-40 minutes until they've visibly increased in size and look nice and puffy.

itemListElement.text: Preheat your oven to 350℉ (180℃).
itemListElement.name: Preheat your oven to 350℉ (180℃).

itemListElement.text: Brush cardamom buns with egg wash.
itemListElement.name: Brush cardamom buns with egg wash.

itemListElement.text: Bake the cardamom buns for 15-20 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 200°F (95°C). Meanwhile, prepare the simple syrup and cardamom sugar:
itemListElement.name: Bake the cardamom buns for 15-20 minutes or until the internal temperature reaches 200°F (95°C). Meanwhile, prepare the simple syrup and cardamom sugar:

itemListElement.text: To make the simple syrup, add ingredients to a small saucepan over medium heat. Stir until it simmers, and continue simmering until the sugar has dissolved. Set aside.
itemListElement.name: To make the simple syrup, add ingredients to a small saucepan over medium heat. Stir until it simmers, and continue simmering until the sugar has dissolved. Set aside.

itemListElement.text: To make the cardamom sugar, grind decorticated cardamom in your mortar and pestle until it’s medium-fine. Then add the sugar and grind for a few additional seconds until combined. Set aside.
itemListElement.name: To make the cardamom sugar, grind decorticated cardamom in your mortar and pestle until it’s medium-fine. Then add the sugar and grind for a few additional seconds until combined. Set aside.

itemListElement.text: After baking, brush the tops of the buns with simple syrup and sprinkle with cardamom sugar. Transfer them to a wire rack to cool for at least 15 minutes. Enjoy!
itemListElement.name: After baking, brush the tops of the buns with simple syrup and sprinkle with cardamom sugar. Transfer them to a wire rack to cool for at least 15 minutes. Enjoy!

review.reviewBody:
These buns are delicious! Made them today and I’m pretty sure I’ll be making them again in the next week. 
Thank you for the clear instructions!
Great recipe - my first time making them and I'm delighted with the results - the dough is beautiful and soft and the cardamom spicing is perfectly nuanced, delicious!!

There're few recipes cardamom bans in Japanese, so I'm really happy you give us this recipe. I wish I could introduce your recipe in Japanese someday because all your recipe must be loved by Japanese bakers...
